evals.prompt: Create an ADR for: we need to choose between DynamoDB and Aurora PostgreSQL for our new order service. It handles 10k orders/day, needs strong consistency for financial data, and will be queried by order ID, customer ID, and date range. Team knows SQL well.
evals.expected_output: An ADR evaluating both options against WA pillars, recommending one with clear trade-off analysis, considering the team's SQL expertise and the query patterns.
assertions: Evaluates both DynamoDB and Aurora PostgreSQL as options
Assesses impact on all 6 WA pillars for each option
Considers the team's SQL expertise as a factor (favoring Aurora)
Addresses the query patterns (date range queries favor relational)
Addresses strong consistency requirement
States explicit trade-offs of the chosen option
Includes review triggers (when to revisit the decision)

evals.prompt: ADR: should we go multi-region active-active or single-region with cross-region backup for our SaaS platform? We have users in US and EU, current monthly revenue $200k, 99.95% SLA commitment.
evals.expected_output: An ADR weighing the significant cost/complexity increase of active-active against the business risk of single-region, tied to the revenue and SLA commitment.
assertions: Evaluates both options (active-active and single-region with backup)
Addresses cost difference between options (active-active is significantly more expensive)
References the $200k revenue and 99.95% SLA as decision-driving factors
Assesses Reliability pillar impact deeply (this is the core trade-off)
Assesses Cost pillar impact (significant difference)
States conditions under which to revisit (revenue growth, SLA increase, outage)
Includes implementation notes with effort and timeline

evals.prompt: Document our decision: we're choosing ECS Fargate over EKS for our new microservices platform. We're a team of 4 with no Kubernetes experience, building 6 services, need to ship MVP in 3 months.
evals.expected_output: An ADR documenting ECS Fargate as the decision, with EKS as the evaluated alternative, clearly showing that team size and timeline drove the choice over raw capability.
assertions: Documents ECS Fargate as the chosen option and EKS as the alternative
Identifies team size (4) and no K8s experience as primary decision drivers
Addresses the 3-month timeline as a constraint favoring simpler option
Does NOT recommend EKS (the decision is already made, document it)
Identifies trade-offs (less ecosystem/flexibility vs faster time-to-market)
Includes review triggers (team growth, need for advanced orchestration, multi-cloud)
Assesses Operational Excellence impact (simpler ops with Fargate for small team)
